english: 22,5, might perh. be rendered: to appease, to pacify. — 2. bz̀ams-bsgó byéd-pa (bzhams bsgo byed pa) Lex. to remind of, to call to mind.
to scrape, with a knife, to shave or shear, with a razor Med.; skra bzár-ba (skra bzhar ba) the hair.

1. four; bz̀i-pa (bzhi pa), bz̀i-po (bzhi po) cf. dgu (dgu); bz̀i-bcu (bzhi bcu) (col. *z̀ib-c̀u* (*zhib chu*)) 40, bz̀i-bcu-rtsa-gcig (bzhi bcu rtsa gcig) (W. *z̀ib-c̀u-z̀e-c̀ig* (*zhib chu zhe cig*)), z̀e-gcig (zhe gcig) etc. the numbers 41—49; bz̀i-brgyá (bzhi brgya) 400, bz̀i-stoṅ (bzhi stong) 4000 etc.; bz̀i-c̀a (bzhi cha) one fourth, a quarter; bz̀i-tsan-gyi-sdé-pa pyed-daṅ-brgyád (bzhi tshan gyi sde pa phyed dang brgyad) the 7 1/2 tetrads (of letters) Gram. — 2. often incorr. for z̀i (zhi) or gz̀i (gzhi).

1. sbst. face, countenance, ráb-tu mi-sdúg-pa (rab tu mi sdug pa) (of) a very ugly face Dzl., légs-pa (legs pa), mdzés-pa (mdzes pa) Glr. (of) a handsome, a pretty face; bz̀in-mdzés-ma (bzhin mdzes ma) a woman or girl with a pretty face; bz̀in zágs-te (bzhin zags te) the face dripping (from perspiration); bz̀in ₀dzúm-pa daṅ bcás-pa (bzhin 'dzum pa dang bcas pa) with a friendly smiling countenance Mil.; bz̀in-pags sér-po (bzhin pags ser po) the skin of the face being yellow (as in bilious complaints) Mng.; bz̀in-rás (bzhin ras) the appearance, ṅán-pa (ngan pa) Med.; bz̀in-bzàṅ (bzhin bzang), fem. bz̀in-bzaṅ-ma (bzhin bzang ma), a polite address: my dear Sir; kye bz̀in-bzaṅ-dag (kye bzhin bzang dag) much respected gentlemen! also in other instances as a word of politeness: bz̀in-bzaṅ-ma dé-dag laṅs-te (bzhin bzang ma de dag langs te) the ladies rose and...; it seems to be particularly in favour, when apparitions are addressed Mil. — 2. particle, the meaning of which corresponds in part to that of the Greek prep. κατά c. acc., gen. used as an adv. bz̀in-du (bzhin du) or bz̀in (bzhin), but also as an adj. with pa (pa): a. joined to verbal roots, bz̀in (bzhin) serves to form with them a partic. pres., and bz̀in-du (bzhin du) a gerund, tugs-mnyés-bz̀in-pai ṅaṅ-la (thugs mnyes bzhin pa'i ngang la) in a rejoicing frame of mind, in a joyful mood Mil.; kŕi-la bzúgs-bz̀in-du (khri la bzhugs bzhin du) sitting on the chair Dzl.; skrág-bz̀in-du (skrag bzhin du) from fear Dzl. (cf. καθ' ὕπνον); mdaṅs gyur bz̀in-du (mdangs gyur bzhin du) whilst his colour changes Dzl.; mi s̀es bz̀in-du s̀es-so z̀es zer (mi shes bzhin du shes so zhes zer) not knowing it he pretends to know it Stg.; dád-bz̀in-du log soṅ (dad bzhin du log song) 'credentes discesserunt', believing they went away Mil. b. bz̀in(-du) (bzhin (du)) as postp. c. acc., agreeably, in conformity, according to, very frq.; c̀os bz̀in-du (chos bzhin du) according to the precepts of religion Dzl. (cf. κατὰ νόμον), rgyál-pos bsgó-ba bz̀in-du sgrúb-pa (rgyal pos bsgo ba bzhin du sgrub pa) to execute a thing according to the king's command, to perform his order frq.; kyod ji-skad smrás-pa bz̀in-du gz̀án-dag-la bsnyád-de (khyod ji skad smras pa bzhin du gzhan dag la bsnyad de) relating to the others according to what has been said by you, = relating what you have said Dzl.; yíd-bz̀in-du (yid bzhin du) to heart's content frq.; like, as, ri gyél-ba (ri gyel ba) like the breaking down of a mountain Dzl.; also with a pleonastic ltar (ltar): mkán-po ji-ltar gsúṅ-ba bz̀in (mkhan po ji ltar gsung ba bzhin) Glr., or, which would be the same, ji-bz̀in gsúṅ-ba ltar (ji bzhin gsung ba ltar), as the very learned gentleman has said, foretold; de bz̀in-du so (de bzhin du so) = dé-ltar (de ltar); de-de-bz̀in-no (de de bzhin no) yes, that is so; de-bz̀in-nyid (de bzhin nyid) (तथता), truth, reality, substance, essentiality Was. (272), identity (297), in mysticism = c̀os-nyíd (chos nyid) Thgy., v. c̀os (chos), comp. c. pyí-bz̀in (-du) (phyi bzhin (du)), pyir-bz̀in(-du) (phyir bzhin (du)) afterwards, subsequently (cf. κατόπισθε). — d. distrib. nyin-ré-bz̀in(-du) (nyin re bzhin (du)), daily, per day (καθ' ἡμέραν), nyin-gcig-bz̀in-du (nyin gcig bzhin du) id.; re-re-bz̀in-gyi mdzad-pa (re re bzhin gyi mdzad pa) Glr. his daily doings.
v. z̀u-ba II. (zhu ba II.) and ju-ba ('ju ba), to melt.

resp. for sdód-pa (sdod pa) and ₀dúg-pa ('dug pa), 1. to sit, bzúgs-su gsol (bzhugs su gsol) B, bzúgs (-z̀u) (bzhugs (zhu)) col., please sit down! — bzúgs-kŕi (bzhugs khri) chair; throne. — 2. to dwell, reside, bzúgs-pai p̀o-bráṅ (bzhugs pa'i pho brang) castle of residence Dzl.; bzúgs-pai rten (bzhugs pa'i rten) a small temple in which a deity resides Dzl.; bzúgs-grógs (bzhugs grogs) fellow-lodger: — 3. to remain, stay, exist, live, ₀jig-rtén-du bzúgs-pa ('jig rten du bzhugs pa) to be in the world, to live on earth, of Buddha and saints; also, still to remain in the world; stón-pa bzúgs-pai dús-su (ston pa bzhugs pa'i dus su) during the life-time of the Teacher (Buddha) Tar.; kyed ₀dir bzúgs (khyed 'dir bzhugs) c̀os-mdzád (chos mdzad) ye devout here present = my devout friends! Mil.; *z̀ug yó-dham* (*zhug yo dham*) C. are you at home? *ku z̀ug naṅ yó-dham* (*ku zhug nang yo dham*) C. are you coming? = welcome! well-met! ; transferred to writings, texts etc., to be contained, so in titles of books: mdzaṅs-blún z̀es-byá-ba bzúgs-so (mdzangs blun zhes bya ba bzhugs so) the so-styled 'Sage and Fool' is contained (in the present volume); bló-la bzúgs-pa (blo la bzhugs pa) daṅ glegs-bám-du bzúgs-pa tams-c̀ád (dang glegs bam du bzhugs pa thams cad) yi-ger spel (yi ger spel) all that was found in the memories (of individual persons) and in books, was recorded Tar.
